basic_details.job_title: KEA KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 1087 Vacancies in Karnataka Residential Schools
basic_details.organization_name: Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA), on behalf of Karnataka Residential Educational Institutions Societies (KREIS)
basic_details.post_name: Teacher (Kannada, English, Hindi, Mathematics, Science, Social Science, Computer Science, Physical Education, Drawing)
basic_details.short_information: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA), Bengaluru, has issued two separate notifications on 10 July 2026 inviting online applications for Teacher posts in residential schools run under the Karnataka Residential Educational Institutions Societies (KREIS) - including Morarji Desai, Kittur Rani Chennamma, Atal Bihari Vajpayee, Smt. Indira Gandhi, and Dr. B.R. Ambedkar residential schools. Together, the two notifications cover 1087 vacancies - 767 posts under the Rest of the State (RPC) cadre and 320 posts under the Kalyana-Karnataka (KK) local cadre. Eligible candidates can apply online throu

application_fee.details: Fee must be paid online only; fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances. | Candidates applying for more than one post can submit a single online application, but must pay the applicable fee for the first post plus an additional ₹100 per additional post applied for.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on the last date of online application submission. As per Government Order No. ಸಿಆಸಇ 262 ಸೇನನಿ 2025 dated 29-01-2026, a one-time relaxation of 5 years has been added to the standard upper age limit under Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977. | Minimum age required is 18 years. Additional age relaxation is available for government/local-body employees, ex-servicemen, National Cadet Corps full-time supervisors, Gram Samuha Parishilakar (village survey/resource person) candidates, special pay candidates, and widows, as per the detailed relaxation clauses in the official notification.

qualification.details: Kannada Language Teachers: Graduation with Kannada as an optional subject in the Arts stream from a recognized university, or the four-year "Kannada Pandith" course from the Karnataka State Pre-University Education Board, together with a Kannada B.Ed. degree; or a Bachelor's degree with Kannada as an optional subject plus a postgraduate degree in Kannada. English Language Teachers: Graduation with English as an optional subject in the Arts stream, or a postgraduate diploma in English from a Regional Institute of English in South India, together with a B.Ed. with English as the primary method subject; or a postgraduate degree in English with Kannada studied as one subject up to degree/PUC/SSLC level, or a Karnataka Pre-University Education Board/Kannada Sahitya Parishat Kannada proficiency certificate (Kaav/Jaan/Ratna), or a Diploma in Kannada. Hindi Language Teachers: Graduation with Hindi as an optional subject and a B.Ed. degree; or a postgraduate/graduate degree with Hindi as the major subject along with a B.Ed. with Hindi as method subject, in addition to a Kannada language proficiency requirement (Kaav/Jaan/Ratna certificate from the Karnataka Pradha and Praudha Shikshana Parishad/Kannada Sahitya Parishat, Bengaluru, or Kannada studied as a subject up to degree/PUC/SSLC level, or a Diploma in Kannada). Mathematics Teachers: Graduation in Science with Mathematics and Physics as combined subjects, with one additional optional subject (e.g., Chemistry), together with a B.Ed. in the Science method; along with the same Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Science Teachers: Graduation in Science with Chemistry as a combined subject along with one of Botany/Zoology/Biology as an additional optional subject, together with a B.Ed. in the Science method; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Social Science Teachers: Graduation with any two of History, Economics, Political Science, Sociology and Geography as optional subjects in the Arts stream, together with a B.Ed. in the Arts method; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Note: Candidates applying for Social Science Teacher posts must have studied in the English medium, since instruction for this subject in the residential schools is in English medium. Computer Science Teachers (RPC cadre only): Graduation in Engineering/Technology with Computer Science, Information Science, Instrumentation, Electronics & Communication, or Agriculture (B.Sc.) related discipline, or a Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA), or B.Sc. with Computer Science and equivalent current qualification, together with a B.Ed. degree with Kannada studied as a subject at degree/PUC/SSLC level, or the diploma/proficiency test alternatives noted above. Physical Education Teachers: A recognized university degree together with a Bachelor of Physical Education (B.P.Ed.) degree; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Graduate Drawing Teachers (KK cadre only): Bachelor's degree in Fine Arts (B.F.A.) subject, or Bachelor's degree in Visual Arts (B.V.A.), or the Art Master certificate examination in Drawing and Painting conducted by the Technical Examination Board as per Government Order No. 27 SLV 2004 dated 21-03-2006 for candidates with specified qualifications in Fine Arts, Commercial/Applied Arts from recognized institutions; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Diploma Drawing Teachers (KK cadre only): Diploma in Drawing/Painting/Commerce/Applied or other Fine Arts from a recognized technical examination board, as per the same Government Order; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. | Additional mandatory qualification: Candidates applying for post serial numbers 1-6 (Kannada, English, Hindi, Mathematics, Science, and Social Science Teachers) must hold a valid Karnataka Teacher Eligibility Test (TET, Paper-2) certificate, or a Central Teacher Eligibility Test (CTET) certificate conducted by CBSE, to be eligible to apply.

salary_details.details: Kannada, English, Hindi, Mathematics, Science, Social Science, Computer Science and Physical Education Teachers: Pay scale of ₹54,175 - ₹99,400 (2024 Revised State Pay Scale). Graduate & Diploma Drawing Teachers: Pay scale of ₹49,050 - ₹92,500 (2024 Revised State Pay Scale). | Selected candidates are covered under the National Pension System (NPS) as per Government Order No. FD(SPL)04 PET 2005 dated 31-03-2006. No GPF scheme applies to society employees, and no PF scheme is applicable.

selection_process: Selection to Teacher posts is based purely on a competitive written examination; there is no interview.

Competitive Written Examination - Offline OMR-based test consisting of Paper-I (General Knowledge) and Paper-II (Subject-specific paper). Document Verification - As per merit and category-wise reservation.

Minimum qualifying marks in the competitive exam is 35% for general category candidates; candidates scoring below this are disqualified for the selection process. Negative marking: Each question has 4 options; 0.25 marks will be deducted for every wrong answer.

pattern_table.Subject: General Knowledge (current affairs, general science, Indian society and its evolution, Indian Constitution and public administration, practical knowledge and intelligence up to SSLC level, Karnataka's social and cultural history, land reforms and social change since independence, Karnataka's economy - strengths and weaknesses, rural development/Panchayat Raj/rural cooperative institutions, Karnataka's efficient use of science and technology, environmental issues, and mental ability)

how_to_apply: Visit the official KEA CET online portal: cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea.
Locate the link for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026 (RPC cadre or Kalyana-Karnataka cadre, as applicable) and register with your basic details.
Fill in the online application form carefully with personal, educational and category details.
Upload your photograph (JPEG, 50KB-200KB) and signature (JPEG, 50KB-70KB) as per the specified format.
Pay the applicable application fee online, including additional fee if applying for more than one post.
Review all entered details before final submission, as no correction is allowed after submission.
Download and print a copy of the submitted application and fee payment receipt/challan for future reference.
important_instructions: Applications can be submitted online only; no offline or postal applications will be accepted. Only one online application can be submitted per post; no modification, addition or correction is permitted after submission under any circumstances. Candidates must ensure all eligibility-related information is filled in correctly before final submission; incomplete or incorrect information can lead to rejection at any stage, even after appointment. Candidates must download and carry the admit card along with one original photo ID proof (PAN card, driving licence, Aadhaar card, passport, voter ID, or government employee ID) to the exam centre; entry without ID proof will not be permitted. The exam centre once allotted cannot be changed under any circumstances. Furnishing false information, forged documents, or impersonation will lead to disqualification and may attract criminal and disciplinary action. The mobile number and email ID provided in the application will be treated as final for all official communication.

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: Two official notifications have been issued by the Karnataka Examination Authority, Bengaluru: Notification No. ಇಡಿ/ಕೆಇಎ1/02/ನೇ.ವಿ/2026(ಆರ್‌ಪಿಸಿ) dated 10-07-2026 - for 767 Rest of the State (RPC) cadre posts Notification No. ಇಡಿ/ಕೆಇಎ1/05/ನೇ.ವಿ/2026(ಕೆ.ಕೆ) dated 10-07-2026 - for 320 Kalyana-Karnataka (KK) local cadre posts Candidates should download and read both notifications carefully from the official KEA website before applying.

rows.details: Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA), on behalf of Karnataka Residential Educational Institutions Societies (KREIS)
Teacher (Kannada, English, Hindi, Mathematics, Science, Social Science, Computer Science, Physical Education, Drawing)

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The 1087 vacancies are notified separately for the Rest of the State (RPC) cadre and the Kalyana-Karnataka (KK) local cadre, with subject-wise distribution as follows. Note: The figures in brackets marked "ಹಿಂ" (HM) refer to backlog Hindi-medium instruction vacancies not filled in earlier recruitment cycles, and are included within the total count of each post. Post/category-wise reservation details will be notified separately by the KREIS society. Note that Computer Science Teacher posts are notified only under the RPC cadre, and Drawing Teacher posts (Graduate & Diploma) are notified only under the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre.

tables.caption: Rest of the State (RPC) Cadre - 767 Posts

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidate must be an Indian citizen. Candidate must be mentally and physically fit to discharge the duties of the post. A male candidate with more than one living spouse, and a female candidate married to such a person, is not eligible for appointment unless specifically exempted by the government.

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Educational Qualification (Subject-wise)
content_sections.text: Kannada Language Teachers: Graduation with Kannada as an optional subject in the Arts stream from a recognized university, or the four-year "Kannada Pandith" course from the Karnataka State Pre-University Education Board, together with a Kannada B.Ed. degree; or a Bachelor's degree with Kannada as an optional subject plus a postgraduate degree in Kannada. English Language Teachers: Graduation with English as an optional subject in the Arts stream, or a postgraduate diploma in English from a Regional Institute of English in South India, together with a B.Ed. with English as the primary method subject; or a postgraduate degree in English with Kannada studied as one subject up to degree/PUC/SSLC level, or a Karnataka Pre-University Education Board/Kannada Sahitya Parishat Kannada proficiency certificate (Kaav/Jaan/Ratna), or a Diploma in Kannada. Hindi Language Teachers: Graduation with Hindi as an optional subject and a B.Ed. degree; or a postgraduate/graduate degree with Hindi as the major subject along with a B.Ed. with Hindi as method subject, in addition to a Kannada language proficiency requirement (Kaav/Jaan/Ratna certificate from the Karnataka Pradha and Praudha Shikshana Parishad/Kannada Sahitya Parishat, Bengaluru, or Kannada studied as a subject up to degree/PUC/SSLC level, or a Diploma in Kannada). Mathematics Teachers: Graduation in Science with Mathematics and Physics as combined subjects, with one additional optional subject (e.g., Chemistry), together with a B.Ed. in the Science method; along with the same Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Science Teachers: Graduation in Science with Chemistry as a combined subject along with one of Botany/Zoology/Biology as an additional optional subject, together with a B.Ed. in the Science method; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Social Science Teachers: Graduation with any two of History, Economics, Political Science, Sociology and Geography as optional subjects in the Arts stream, together with a B.Ed. in the Arts method; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Note: Candidates applying for Social Science Teacher posts must have studied in the English medium, since instruction for this subject in the residential schools is in English medium. Computer Science Teachers (RPC cadre only): Graduation in Engineering/Technology with Computer Science, Information Science, Instrumentation, Electronics & Communication, or Agriculture (B.Sc.) related discipline, or a Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA), or B.Sc. with Computer Science and equivalent current qualification, together with a B.Ed. degree with Kannada studied as a subject at degree/PUC/SSLC level, or the diploma/proficiency test alternatives noted above. Physical Education Teachers: A recognized university degree together with a Bachelor of Physical Education (B.P.Ed.) degree; along with the Kannada proficiency requirement as above. Graduate Drawing Teachers (KK cadre only): Bachelor's degree

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Age Limit 2026
content_sections.text: Age is calculated as on the last date of online application submission. As per Government Order No. ಸಿಆಸಇ 262 ಸೇನನಿ 2025 dated 29-01-2026, a one-time relaxation of 5 years has been added to the standard upper age limit under Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977. Minimum age required is 18 years. Additional age relaxation is available for government/local-body employees, ex-servicemen, National Cadet Corps full-time supervisors, Gram Samuha Parishilakar (village survey/resource person) candidates, special pay candidates, and widows, as per the detailed relaxation clauses in the official notification.

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Kannada, English, Hindi, Mathematics, Science, Social Science, Computer Science and Physical Education Teachers: Pay scale of ₹54,175 - ₹99,400 (2024 Revised State Pay Scale). Graduate & Diploma Drawing Teachers: Pay scale of ₹49,050 - ₹92,500 (2024 Revised State Pay Scale). Selected candidates are covered under the National Pension System (NPS) as per Government Order No. FD(SPL)04 PET 2005 dated 31-03-2006. No GPF scheme applies to society employees, and no PF scheme is applicable.

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: Fee must be paid online only; fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances. Candidates applying for more than one post can submit a single online application, but must pay the applicable fee for the first post plus an additional ₹100 per additional post applied for.

rows.category: SC / ST / Category-1 / Ex-servicemen / Third Gender

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection to Teacher posts is based purely on a competitive written examination; there is no interview. Competitive Written Examination - Offline OMR-based test consisting of Paper-I (General Knowledge) and Paper-II (Subject-specific paper). Document Verification - As per merit and category-wise reservation. Minimum qualifying marks in the competitive exam is 35% for general category candidates; candidates scoring below this are disqualified for the selection process. Negative marking: Each question has 4 options; 0.25 marks will be deducted for every wrong answer.

rows.subject: General Knowledge (current affairs, general science, Indian society and its evolution, Indian Constitution and public administration, practical knowledge and intelligence up to SSLC level, Karnataka's social and cultural history, land reforms and social change since independence, Karnataka's economy - strengths and weaknesses, rural development/Panchayat Raj/rural cooperative institutions, Karnataka's efficient use of science and technology, environmental issues, and mental ability)

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Kannada Language Test
content_sections.text: Candidates who have not studied Kannada as a first or second language up to SSLC level, or an equivalent level recognized by the state government, or who have not passed a Kannada test conducted by a competent authority, will be required to appear for a qualifying Kannada Language Test after the main exam. This test carries 150 marks, and candidates must secure a minimum of 50 marks to qualify; marks scored are not counted in the final merit list, and candidates scoring below the minimum become ineligible for appointment.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official KEA CET online portal: cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea . Locate the link for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026 (RPC cadre or Kalyana-Karnataka cadre, as applicable) and register with your basic details. Fill in the online application form carefully with personal, educational and category details. Upload your photograph (JPEG, 50KB-200KB) and signature (JPEG, 50KB-70KB) as per the specified format. Pay the applicable application fee online, including additional fee if applying for more than one post. Review all entered details before final submission, as no correction is allowed after submission. Download and print a copy of the submitted application and fee payment receipt/challan for future reference.

content_sections.heading: KREIS Teacher Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Applications can be submitted online only; no offline or postal applications will be accepted. Only one online application can be submitted per post; no modification, addition or correction is permitted after submission under any circumstances. Candidates must ensure all eligibility-related information is filled in correctly before final submission; incomplete or incorrect information can lead to rejection at any stage, even after appointment. Candidates must download and carry the admit card along with one original photo ID proof (PAN card, driving licence, Aadhaar card, passport, voter ID, or government employee ID) to the exam centre; entry without ID proof will not be permitted. The exam centre once allotted cannot be changed under any circumstances. Furnishing false information, forged documents, or impersonation will lead to disqualification and may attract criminal and disciplinary action. The mobile number and email ID provided in the application will be treated as final for all official communication.

faq.question: Q1. How many total vacancies are there in KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 1087 total vacancies - 767 posts under the Rest of the State (RPC) cadre and 320 posts under the Kalyana-Karnataka (KK) local cadre.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window opens on 15 July 2026 and closes on 13 August 2026, with the fee payment deadline on 14 August 2026.

faq.question: Q3. Is TET or CTET mandatory for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates applying for Kannada, English, Hindi, Mathematics, Science, and Social Science Teacher posts must hold a valid Karnataka TET (Paper-2) or CTET certificate. This requirement is not specified for Computer Science, Physical Education, and Drawing Teacher posts in the notification.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years. The maximum age is 45 years for General category, 48 years for Category 2A/2B/3A/3B, and 50 years for SC/ST/Category-1, with additional relaxations applicable for specific categories.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for KREIS Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹750 for General and 2A/2B/3A/3B candidates, ₹500 for SC/ST/Category-1/Ex-servicemen/Third gender candidates, and ₹250 for special pay (PWD) candidates. An additional ₹100 applies per extra post if applying for more than one.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an interview in the KREIS Teacher selection process?
faq.answer: No, selection is based purely on the competitive written examination followed by document verification. There is no interview.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the KREIS Teacher exam?
faq.answer: Yes, 0.25 marks will be deducted for each wrong answer in the OMR-based competitive exam.

faq.question: Q8. What is the pay scale for KREIS Teacher posts?
faq.answer: Most Teacher posts carry a pay scale of ₹54,175 - ₹99,400, while Graduate and Diploma Drawing Teacher posts carry a pay scale of ₹49,050 - ₹92,500, as per the 2024 Revised State Pay Scale.

faq.question: Q9. Is the Kannada language test compulsory for all candidates?
faq.answer: No. It applies only to candidates who have not studied Kannada as a first or second language up to SSLC or an equivalent level. It is qualifying in nature (minimum 50 out of 150 marks) and marks are not added to the final merit.

faq.question: Q10. Are Computer Science and Drawing Teacher posts available in both cadres?
faq.answer: No. Computer Science Teacher posts are notified only under the RPC cadre, while Graduate and Diploma Drawing Teacher posts are notified only under the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre.
